spec_match.rationale: The concept now demonstrates reduced User-Agent parsing limits, low-entropy UA-CH, high-entropy getHighEntropyValues requests, and the matching HTTP Client Hints opt-in path.

interactive.rationale: The page has live controls for refreshing browser signals, choosing high-entropy hints, calling the JavaScript API, and fetching a scoped server echo route.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature blurb promises migration from User-Agent parsing to UA-CH, and the page now provides a working migration console for that exact flow.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo surfaces unsupported navigator.userAgentData fallback, empty high-entropy selections, missing high-entropy request headers, and a handled failed fetch route.

references.rationale: The see-also section retains the required https://chromestatus.com/feature/6224706757853184 link and adds UA-CH implementation/spec references.

design_system.rationale: The concept uses CSS variables, keeps local button background and color overrides paired, and replaces the static snippet-card pattern with live controls.

summary: The UA-CH migration concept now answers the prior open questions with a live reduced-UA and Client Hints migration console, scoped header echo route, graceful fallback paths, and request/response header inspection.
